stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags, including 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The pipeline indicates its primary use case is text generation.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of transformer-based text generation models.
The tags indicate relevance to major AI frameworks like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Cited but Not Verified: Parsing and Evaluating Source Attribution in LLM Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Corey Feld
bullet_points: LLM-powered research agents struggle with verifiable source attribution, despite synthesizing information from numerous web sources.
Existing methods are flawed, either trusting potentially biased self-citation or using RAG without validating source accessibility or factual consistency.
The paper introduces a novel source attribution evaluation framework utilizing a reproducible AST parser to address these verification gaps.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags including transformers and pytorch.
The pipeline demonstrated is text-generation, indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models come in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.
The partnership involves Ollama, OpenAI, and ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools).

stories.title: All the latest updates on AI data centers
stories.author: Justine Calma
bullet_points: AI data centers are the critical physical infrastructure supporting the expansion of AI technologies.
The rapid build-out of these massive, energy-intensive facilities is causing global contention.
Key concerns surrounding data center expansion include strain on power grids, environmental impact, and community opposition.

stories.title: Course correction: Google to link more sources in AI Overviews
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is enhancing its AI Overviews feature by improving source citation.
This change aims to provide users with more transparent and traceable information from the search results.
The update represents a course correction in how Google integrates and attributes information from various web sources.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method designed for fine-grained control in video generation, addressing both actor motion and camera cinematography.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a new scene while allowing per-frame control over intrinsic and extrinsic camera parameters.
The method leverages any pre-trained image-to-video diffusion model that accepts scene depth and camera conditioning.

stories.title: AI Co-Mathematician: Accelerating Mathematicians with Agentic AI
stories.author: Pushmeet Kohli
bullet_points: Introduction of an 'AI co-mathematician,' a workbench designed to aid mathematicians in open-ended research.
The system provides holistic support across the entire mathematical workflow, from ideation and literature search to theorem proving.
Key features include an asynchronous, stateful workspace that manages uncertainty and tracks failed hypotheses.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method designed for fine-grained control in video generation, addressing both character motion and camera cinematography.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a novel scene while allowing per-frame control over camera parameters (intrinsic and extrinsic).
The model is built upon existing pretrained image-to-video diffusion models, conditioned on scene depth and other inputs.

stories.title: Why Global LLM Leaderboards Are Misleading: Small Portfolios for Heterogeneous Supervised ML
stories.author: Swati Gupta
bullet_points: The study challenges the validity of current global LLM leaderboards, which rely on pairwise human feedback (e.g., Arena).
Analysis of ~89K comparisons across 52 LLMs shows that the standard Bradley-Terry (BT) ranking is misleading.
The findings indicate that the top-performing models are statistically indistinguishable, with win probabilities barely exceeding 0.53 within the top tier.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of current retrieval-augmented agents treating information retrieval as a 'black box'.
Current methods rely on iterative, exploratory querying, mimicking a novice user rather than an expert.
The proposed 'Superintelligent Retrieval Agent' aims to improve efficiency by incorporating strong priors, reducing latency and unnecessary retrieval rounds.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model execution and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Amazon is adding a vertical video feed to Prime Video
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Amazon Prime Video is integrating a vertical, short-form video feed called "Clips."
This feature mirrors trends set by competitors like Netflix and Disney Plus.
Users can watch clips and then transition to renting or purchasing the full content.

stories.title: Here is Yarbo’s promise to fix the robot mower that ran me over
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Robot lawn mowers from Yarbo have been vulnerable to hacking.
The vulnerability allows hackers to access sensitive personal data like GPS coordinates and Wi-Fi passwords.
Yarbo has issued a statement promising a fix for the security flaw.

stories.title: The new Wild West of AI kids’ toys
stories.author: Sophie Charara, WIRED.com
bullet_points: New connected AI companions are emerging in the children's toy market.
These toys have the potential to significantly disrupt traditional play and family routines.
The rise of these devices is prompting legislative concern, with calls for potential bans.

stories.title: Manufacturing qubits that can move
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: The article discusses the technical challenge of integrating electronic manufacturing with flexible qubit geometries.
This points to ongoing hurdles in scaling and fabricating advanced quantum computing hardware.
The topic is relevant to the cutting edge of quantum technology development.

stories.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts learning platform Canvas amid finals
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A cyberattack has disrupted the widely used learning platform, Canvas.
The disruption occurred during a critical period, specifically amid year-end finals.
The incident forced numerous schools and colleges across the country to postpone their final examinations.

stories.title: Elon Musk faces criminal probe in France after ignoring summons in X case
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Elon Musk is facing a criminal probe in France.
The probe relates to an unspecified 'X case'.
France has threatened criminal charges if Musk fails to appear for questioning.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: UniPool proposes a new approach to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures, challenging the conventional rigid per-layer expert allocation.
The research suggests that the assumption of isolated expert capacity per layer may be overly restrictive, as evidenced by routing probe results.
The study indicates that replacing a layer's learned top-k router with uniform random routing results in only minor accuracy drops (1.0-1.6 points).

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation of LLMs: their inability to generate novel, valid, and challenging mathematical problems for training.
Introduces VHG, a new method that uses verification to ensure the generated problems are mathematically sound, overcoming issues with naive self-play.
The research is relevant to advancing autonomous scientific research and improving the robustness of LLMs in complex reasoning tasks.

stories.title: Optimizer-Model Consistency: Full Finetuning with the Same Optimizer as Pretraining Forgets Less
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The research investigates the role of optimizers in Large Language Model (LLM) training, specifically comparing pretraining and finetuning.
The study observes that using the exact same optimizer during full finetuning as was used during pretraining results in a better learning-forgetting tradeoff.
This approach reportedly outperforms other optimizers and even LoRA during Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) by minimizing knowledge forgetting.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no pre-existing labeled benchmarks are available.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' for real-world deployments.
Formalizes the necessary conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) for scenario-based audits to serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: Current retrieval-augmented agents often treat information retrieval as a 'black box' process.
Existing methods rely on iterative, exploratory querying, mimicking a novice user's search pattern.
The proposed advancement aims to move beyond black-box retrieval by incorporating expert-level priors to improve efficiency and reduce latency.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the ambiguity in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG) progress, questioning if reported gains are genuine or due to inconsistent evaluation.
Current MMDG research is noted as being fragmented, with significant variations across datasets, modality configurations, and experimental setups.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for being too narrow, focusing heavily on action recognition while neglecting other critical real-world applications.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-horizon decision-making in LLM-based agents, where current methods are too reactive.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework to explicitly incorporate trajectory-level strategy into agentic RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision-making processes.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a novel approach to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
Challenges the conventional practice where each transformer layer maintains its own isolated expert set.
Suggests that decoupling expert capacity from layer depth via shared pooling can maintain performance.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation of LLMs: their inability to generate novel, valid, and challenging problems for scientific advancement.
Introduces VHG, a new method that uses verification to generate hard problems, overcoming issues with naive self-play and reward hacking.
The research is relevant to advancing LLM training and enabling autonomous scientific research by improving problem generation quality.

stories.title: Optimizer-Model Consistency: Full Finetuning with the Same Optimizer as Pretraining Forgets Less
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The paper investigates the role of optimizers in LLM training, specifically comparing pretraining and finetuning.
It observes that using the same optimizer during full finetuning as was used during pretraining leads to a better learning-forgetting tradeoff.
This approach reportedly outperforms other optimizers and LoRA during Supervised Finetuning (SFT).

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established, labeled benchmarks exist for a specific context.
Proposes a framework for 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' to allow for pre-labeling evaluations.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of clarity regarding genuine progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG).
Current MMDG research suffers from fragmentation due to inconsistent evaluation protocols across studies.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for focusing too heavily on action recognition while neglecting broader real-world applications.

stories.title: EMO: Pretraining Mixture of Experts for Emergent Modularity
stories.author: Sewon Min
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inefficiency of monolithic Large Language Models (LLMs) by focusing on Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
A key problem identified is that restricting MoE inference to a subset of experts for specific domains causes severe performance degradation.
The research proposes 'EMO' (Pretraining Mixture of Experts) as a solution to improve the practical applicability and performance of MoEs in memory-constrained scenarios.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: LLMs excel at solving problems but struggle to generate novel, valid, and challenging problems for training.
The paper introduces VHG, a new method to generate hard mathematical problems backed by verification.
VHG aims to overcome limitations of existing methods, such as reliance on human experts or flawed self-play paradigms.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the practical challenge of comparing LLM safety when no established ground-truth benchmarks exist for a specific context.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' for real-world deployment auditing.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can be considered valid deployment evidence.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of optimizing LLMs for long-horizon decision-making in agentic RL.
Introduces StraTA, a framework that incorporates explicit trajectory-level strategy into RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision trajectories.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Optimization
stories.author: Graham Neubig
bullet_points: Introduces Recursive Agent Optimization (RAO), a new RL approach for training self-spawning agents.
Recursive agents use a divide-and-conquer strategy to handle longer contexts and complex problems.
RAO trains models to effectively leverage this recursive, inference-time scaling capability.

stories.title: Can RL Teach Long-Horizon Reasoning to LLMs? Expressiveness Is Key
stories.author: Abulhair Saparov
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in applying RL to LLMs: the lack of controlled, scalable environments for studying reasoning scaling.
Introduces 'ScaleLogic,' a synthetic framework designed to independently control two critical aspects of reasoning difficulty: proof planning depth (horizon) and logical expressiveness.
Focuses on advancing the understanding of how LLMs can learn long-horizon reasoning capabilities using Reinforcement Learning.

stories.title: MASPO: Joint Prompt Optimization for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Min Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MASPO, a new framework for optimizing prompts in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS).
Addresses the challenge of aligning local agent objectives with overall system goals in complex collaborative tasks.
Focuses on the difficulty of jointly optimizing role-specific prompts across interacting agents.

stories.title: Why Most Browser AI Demos Fail on Real Hardware
stories.author: bruno_juca_7038c22bcca1db
bullet_points: The article addresses the gap between AI demos and real-world performance.
It focuses on the challenges of deploying adaptive local AI inference.
The core issue is optimizing AI models for diverse, non-benchmark hardware.

stories.title: Bulletproofing LLM Structured Output in Python: Healing Retries, Cost Caps, and Drift Detection (Runnable Code)
stories.author: velsof
bullet_points: The article addresses practical challenges in deploying LLM structured output in production.
Key topics covered include implementing healing retries, managing cost caps, and detecting output drift.
It provides runnable code examples, indicating a high level of technical utility for developers.

stories.title: I shipped a Notion stack on Gumroad using only Claude Code — here's the build log
stories.author: relayhop
bullet_points: Demonstrates end-to-end development (scraping, ML scoring, deployment) using only Claude Code.
The project involves complex tasks like scraping Gumroad data and applying a 4-dimensional ensemble scoring model.
Highlights the potential for AI tools to automate entire product creation and deployment pipelines.
